exemplar.title: How does Gabriel Garcia creatively use literary devices to delve into societal pressures surrounding female virginity as a symbol of honor in his text Chronicle of a Death Foretold?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does Gabriel Garcia creatively use literary devices to delve into societal pressures surrounding female virginity as a symbol of honor in his text Chronicle of a Death Foretold?

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: Quotations from the novella anchor each paragraph and support the line of inquiry.
Demonstrates a sound grasp of the central issue (virginity and honour) and accurately recounts key events.
weaknesses: Inconsistent and sometimes imprecise citation of quotations (truncated quotes, varied referencing formats).
Interpretations remain fairly surfaceâlevel and lack sustained nuance or depth.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band 3 (Satisfactory): The candidate shows generally relevant textual support and a clear understanding of the work, but missteps in citation and only surfaceâlevel interpretation prevent higher marks.

strengths: Identification and discussion of key devices (diction, characterization, irony) show movement beyond pure summary.
Basic commentary links authorsâ choices to thematic concerns.
weaknesses: Analysis often drifts into description or moral judgement rather than probing authorial intent.
Stylistic terms are sometimes misapplied and analytical depth is limited.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Raised to Band 3: While analysis remains uneven and at times descriptive, there is generally appropriate evaluation of textual features and some attempt to tie technique to meaning.

strengths: Clear overall structure with an introduction, coherent body sections, and a conclusion.
Effective use of topic sentences and transitions to guide the reader through the argument.
weaknesses: Some paragraphs meander or repeat earlier points rather than develop new analysis.
Conclusion restates rather than synthesizes or extends the line of inquiry.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band 4: The essay is well organized and mostly cohesive, with an adequately developed line of inquiry and generally effective integration of examples.

strengths: Language is clear and meaning is seldom obscured.
Appropriate academic vocabulary and generally well-formed sentence structures.
weaknesses: Noticeable lapses in tense, missing articles, and occasional awkward phrasing.
Inconsistent citation formatting and punctuation errors detract from overall precision.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band 3: Language clarity and accuracy are adequate despite lapses, and register is mostly appropriate, but errors prevent a higher band.

strandSelections.reasoning:
Quotations are usually embedded within sentences and followed by commentary, showing conscious integration (e.g., analysing âHe grabbed my whole pussyâ directly after presenting it).  Occasionally, however, long excerpts are dropped in or set off awkwardly, and the parenthetical citations are inconsistent.

Because the majority of examples are linked to the surrounding analysis but not yet seamlessly woven, the work meets Band 3: âSupporting examples sometimes integrated into sentences and paragraphs.â

The student demonstrates a sound grasp of the central issueâthe link between virginity and honourâand accurately recounts key events (Angelaâs rejection, Divina Florâs harassment, the forced marriage to Bayardo).  Interpretations are generally valid, showing how these moments expose patriarchal power.  

However, the discussion remains fairly surface-level.  Broader thematic claims (e.g. âwomen are weak and unable to challenge normsâ) are stated rather than probed, and contextual mis-steps such as calling the novel âChroniclesâ or omitting the authorâs full surname signal only satisfactoryânot sustainedâunderstanding.  Depth of insight into the cultural code of honour, magical-realist tone, or narrative structure is limited.

Overall, knowledge and understanding are solid but not consistently nuanced or persuasive, matching the descriptor for Band 3 (Satisfactory).

The essay follows a recognizable structure: introduction with line of inquiry, three body sections (diction, characterization, irony), and a conclusion.  Topic sentences and transitional phrases provide an adequate logical thread, allowing the reader to follow the argument.

Development of the line of inquiry is uneven.  Some paragraphs meander or repeat points, and the conclusion largely restates rather than extends findings.  Still, the organization is more than basic; ideas build in a coherent sequence that addresses the research question with reasonable clarity.

This aligns with the Band 3 descriptor: âadequately organized with some development of line of inquiry.â

Quotations from the novella appear in every paragraph and are generally on-point: Divina Florâs assault, Angelaâs attempted suicide, Bayardoâs return of the bride, etc.  These references anchor each claim and make the line of inquiry visible.

Nevertheless, textual support is not always handled accuratelyâsome quotations are truncated, page numbers vary (âGarcia 12â vs GarcÃ­a MÃ¡rquez 2014), and one citation (âhe didnât give it to meâ) is partially misquoted.  Secondary material is minimal and the Atwood reference is tangential.  

Because the evidence is âgenerally relevant and mostly supports the ideasâ but not fully precise or sophisticated, Band 3 is the best fit.

The student maintains an academic register, employing analytical verbs and avoiding personal anecdotes.  Quotations containing strong language are necessary for analysis rather than gratuitous, preserving appropriate tone.  

Minor inconsistencies appearâinformal phrases like âgets what he wantsâ or the stand-alone âpussyâ in quotation marks influence tone, and citation style is uneven.  Still, these slips do not undermine the fundamentally formal style expected at HL.

Hence, register and style are âmostly appropriate to the task,â warranting Band 3.

Overall language is clear and the meaning is seldom obscured.  Vocabulary is appropriately academic ("patriarchal", "juxtaposition", "objectifying") and sentences are generally well-formed.

There are, however, noticeable lapses: tense shifts, missing articles (âChronicles of a Death Foretoldâ), awkward phrasing (âBayardo San is characterized as a dominant figure in the contextâ), and occasional punctuation errors.  These flaws detract but do not seriously impede comprehension.

The balance of clarity to error sits squarely in Band 3: clear with adequate accuracy despite lapses.

The essay identifies devicesâdiction, characterization, ironyâand offers some basic commentary (ââgrabbedâ shows brutalityâ; Bayardo is âdominantâ).  These observations move beyond pure summary and begin to link technique to theme.

Yet the analysis frequently drifts into description or moral judgement ("readers feel frustrated") rather than probing how the authorâs narrative choices craft meaning.  Stylistic terms are sometimes misapplied (symbolic names called âintentionalâ¦ to symbolize women as valuableâ without further elaboration).  The exploration of irony is stated rather than analysed, and no attention is paid to GarcÃ­a MÃ¡rquezâs narrative voice or structure.

Given that the work shows "some appropriate analysis but reliant on description," Band 2 is appropriate.

annotations.comment: The line of inquiry is focused and appropriately narrow, clearly defining the textual scope and analytical lens, which supports a coherent organization of the essay. Maintain this clarity throughout to guide a sustained line of inquiry.
The authorâs name should include the diacritical marks (âGabriel GarcÃ­a MÃ¡rquezâ) to reflect accurate textual information and demonstrate attention to authorial context.
The essay refers to the novel as âChronicles of a Death Foretoldâ and cites 1981 inconsistently; correct title singular and clarify publication context to enhance accuracy and credibility.

The Margaret Atwood reference on The Handmaidâs Tale is only tangentially linked and isnât integrated into your line of inquiry; consider removing or more clearly connecting it to your central analysis.
Citations fluctuate between âGarciaâ and âGarcÃ­a MÃ¡rquezâ and lack a consistent format; adopt a single citation style and apply accents uniformly to strengthen scholarly precision.
Labeling âgrabbedâ as simply âinformal dictionâ lacks depth; elaborate on how this word choice contributes to tone or characterization to deepen your analysis.
The discussion of irony in Divina Florâs scene is superficial; explore how the author structures situational irony and its effect on reader expectations to yield more insightful evaluation.
The symbolism claim for the names âDivina Florâ and âHortensiaâ requires textual evidence of authorial intent or cultural connotation to support this interpretation convincingly.
Quotation punctuation around âhe didnât give it to meâ is misaligned; ensure opening and closing quotation marks accurately reflect the original text to maintain textual integrity.
The quotation âIt was Angela who didnât want to marry himâ lacks a parenthetical citation with a page number; include precise citation details for transparency.

This section often shifts into moral commentary about reader frustration rather than analysing Gabriel GarcÃ­a MÃ¡rquezâs narrative techniques; focus on how specific stylistic choices generate these responses.
Frequent use of passive constructions (e.g., âBayardo San is characterizedâ) weakens analytical voice; use active verbs to clearly attribute actions to the author or characters.
Some quotations (e.g., âSheâs all set to be hooked upâ) are dropped into the text without integration; embed quotes within your own sentences to improve flow and cohesion.
The exploration of stigma through irony here is descriptive rather than analytical; demonstrate how the authorâs ironic tone or structure shapes thematic development to strengthen evaluation.
The segment beginning âThereâ appears cut off and out of context; ensure quotations are complete to preserve meaning and support your analysis accurately.
Several paragraphs reiterate previous observations rather than advancing new arguments; aim to introduce fresh analytical angles in each section to develop a sustained inquiry.

The conclusion largely restates prior points instead of synthesizing insights or reflecting on broader implications; consider adding a final evaluative judgment or connection to contemporary contexts.
The novel title should be italicized, and the authorâs name should appear as âGabriel GarcÃ­a MÃ¡rquezâ; correct these formatting and accuracy issues to enhance scholarly tone.

Describing women as âweak and unable to challenge normsâ oversimplifies complex character agency; consider qualifying this claim with textual nuance or counterexamples to avoid broad generalizations.
The Buiano hydrangea care guide is unrelated to literary analysis; remove this entry to maintain relevance and coherence in your reference list.
The URL citation lacks an access date and is formatted inconsistently; revise to include a retrieval date and adhere to your chosen citation style uniformly.
The GarcÃ­a MÃ¡rquez reference omits translator credentials details and does not specify the edition; include full publication data to support precise scholarship.
The opening assertion about womenâs worth being determined by purity is broad; support this claim with specific textual examples or critical scholarship to ground your argument.
Terms like âobjectifyingâ should be linked explicitly to literary device definitions; define how this rhetorical choice aligns with objectification theory to deepen the analysis.
Characterization analysis relies heavily on plot summary; illustrate how the authorâs narrative perspective and tone shape character portrayal rather than recounting events.
Irony examples are cited but lack discussion of their placement within narrative structure; comment on how timing and context of these ironies enhance thematic resonance.
The essay overlooks GarcÃ­a MÃ¡rquezâs magicalârealist style and narrative framing; consider addressing these to enrich your understanding of authorial choices.
